nodes.label: clear
nodes.summary: This project implements a **smart reset mechanism** for an AI assistant's conversation session. It uses a central *orchestrator* to wipe chat history, reset application state, and scrub internal caches, while intelligently identifying and preserving **background tasks** so long-running processes continue uninterrupted.

children.label: Command Definition & Routing
children.summary:
The interface layer that exposes the "clear" functionality to the CLI framework.
It serves as the menu item on a restaurant menu; it defines the command's name, aliases (like "reset"), and metadata, and uses lazy-loading to ensure the heavy lifting code is only imported when the user actually orders it.

children.label: Conversation Clearing Orchestrator
children.summary:
The central coordinator that manages the lifecycle of resetting a user session.
It acts like a factory manager shutting down the production line to retool. It ensures that running processes are handled correctly, hooks are fired, caches are cleared, and a fresh session ID is generated, ensuring a clean slate without crashing the application.

children.label: Background Task Preservation
children.summary:
Logic that determines which processes survive a session reset and which are terminated.
It functions like a lifeboat selection process: "foreground" tasks (active interactions) are terminated to clear the stage, while "background" tasks (long-running agents) are identified and preserved so they can continue working seamlessly across the session boundary.

children.label: App State Reset
children.summary:
The state manipulation logic that physically updates the application's React-like state tree.
It operates like formatting a hard drive but keeping the operating system installed; it removes message history, resets file pointers, and clears metadata, while carefully retaining necessary configuration for preserved background tasks.

children.label: Global Cache Eviction
children.summary:
A dedicated utility for wiping internal memory caches to prevent stale data leakage.
Think of it as a deep cleaning crew that scrubs every surface—wiping Git status, LSP diagnostics, skill caches, and tool definitions—so the new session doesn't "remember" irrelevant context from the previous conversation.
